problem.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
problem.description:
Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays.
                
Constraints:

nums1.length + nums2.length >= 1
Time complexity should be O(log(m+n))
assumptions: Use binary search on the smaller array.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Merge and Find (Not Optimal)
solutions.description: Merge the arrays and find the median. This doesn't meet the O(log(m+n)) requirement but is straightforward.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Binary Search (Optimal) [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use binary search to find the correct partition that gives us the median.

solutions.title: Solution 3 – Find Kth Element [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Find the kth smallest element in the two sorted arrays.

solutions.title: Solution 4 – Iterative Kth Element [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Iterative version to avoid recursion stack space.

solutions.title: Edge Cases and Validation
